notes: A and O are contradictory because the O form is the negation of the A form: not forall x (A->B) == exists x not(A->B) == exists x (A and not B), via thm:qneg. The two forms (def:categorical-a-form, def:categorical-o-form) and the quantifier negation law are all load-bearing and present. The propositional rewrite not(p->q) == p and not-q is an additional ambient step (no clean node, cf. 0022).
